assertions.notes: With-skill uses cn() (twMerge + clsx). Without-skill only suggests clsx, not cn()/twMerge.
With-skill recommends cva() for variants. Without-skill recommends @apply instead.
With-skill explicitly discourages @apply for variants. Without-skill recommends @apply.
With-skill specifies exact order: layout > sizing > spacing > typography > visual > interactive > dark > responsive. Without-skill gives vague grouping advice.
With-skill explicitly mentions tailwind-merge for conflict resolution. Without-skill does not.
Both correctly identify mobile-first approach. General Tailwind knowledge covers this.
Both show responsive text scaling. With-skill uses the exact pattern text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl.
With-skill mentions container queries with @container. Without-skill does not mention them.
With-skill flags p-5 and mb-7 as non-standard. Without-skill says they're 'fine to use'.
Both suggest text-base over text-[16px], though without-skill is less emphatic.
With-skill recommends size-12 and size-8. Without-skill does not mention size-* shorthand.
Both recommend semantic tokens over raw colors.
Both show CSS variable-based theming.
With-skill uses OKLCH format throughout. Without-skill uses hex colors.
With-skill mentions color-mix() in oklab. Without-skill does not mention it.
With-skill uses --color-*: initial to clear defaults. Without-skill does not mention it.
With-skill uses @theme block. Without-skill uses tailwind.config.js extend approach.
Both add focus:ring-2. With-skill also adds focus:ring-offset-2 consistently.
With-skill flags 44x44px minimum and uses size-11. Without-skill does not mention touch targets.
Both add aria-label. With-skill also shows sr-only pattern.
Both add motion-reduce variant.
With-skill adds contrast-more: variant. Without-skill does not mention it.
With-skill explicitly flags transition-all with w/h and recommends GPU properties. Without-skill mentions reflow but doesn't strongly prohibit it.
With-skill shows full pattern with animate-pulse + rounded-md + bg-gray-200 dark:bg-gray-800. Without-skill shows animate-pulse but misses dark mode colors.
With-skill shows @starting-style for popover animations. Without-skill suggests Framer Motion or CSS transitions.
With-skill defines --animate-* and @keyframes in @theme. Without-skill does not mention @theme tokens.
With-skill shows @custom-variant dark (&:where(.dark, .dark *)). Without-skill is vague about v4 dark mode config.
Both show .dark {} block for overriding variables.
With-skill sets bg-background text-foreground on body and border-border on *. Without-skill does not mention this pattern.
Both correctly replace @tailwind with @import 'tailwindcss'.
Both move theme to @theme {} in CSS.
Both mention automatic content detection.
Both replace bg-opacity-50 with /50 modifier.
With-skill explicitly replaces tailwindcss-animate with native CSS. Without-skill says 'check for v4-compatible versions'.
With-skill shows @utility directive. Without-skill does not mention it.
With-skill shows @custom-variant. Without-skill does not mention it.
With-skill shows @tailwindcss/postcss and @tailwindcss/vite with code. Without-skill only vaguely mentions 'PostCSS plugin or Vite plugin'.
With-skill flags node_modules in content paths as problematic. Without-skill does not flag it.
With-skill adds min-w-0 + truncate. Without-skill adds overflow-hidden + truncate but misses min-w-0.
Both use aspect-video + object-cover.
Both recommend systematic z-index values and flag arbitrary values.
Both mention truncate. With-skill also covers line-clamp-N for multi-line.
With-skill shows compound component pattern (Dialog.Trigger, Dialog.Content). Without-skill keeps monolithic structure.
With-skill recommends Radix primitives with code. Without-skill does not mention Radix.
With-skill flags forwardRef as unnecessary in React 19. Without-skill keeps forwardRef.
Both recommend Zod + React Hook Form.
With-skill warns against dynamic class concatenation with string map solution. Without-skill does not address it.
